data.title: The AI investment trend ‘appears intact’ despite recent market turbulence – strategist
data.description:
Dan Greenhaus, chief strategist at Solas Alternative Asset Management, believes the mid-month “AI freak out” in the markets may be over, pointing to strong earnings reports from key technology companies.

In an interview with CNBC, Greenhaus emphasized that Nvidia’s (NVDA) latest results were “quite good,” with other companies tangentially exposed to AI also reporting strong performance.

“I think you just had a moment where people decided to exercise some selling pressure,” Greenhaus explained. “And to me, it was momentary. It’s not emblematic of anything longer lasting.”

The strategist doesn’t anticipate significant changes in market dynamics heading into 2026, noting that the two main pillars of the current market – AI fundamentals and potential Fed rate cuts – are working in investors’ favor.

When questioned about the timeline for AI profitability beyond current capital expenditures, Greenhaus acknowledged the concern but pointed to early signs of business impact.

“I read a bunch of conference calls, listened to a bunch where they talked about designing a marketing campaign… there was also a company that talked about designing a chemical product using proprietary corporate data and AI to design a brand-new product,” he said.

Greenhaus drew parallels to the early internet era, suggesting that while some AI investments may not pan out, they are laying groundwork for substantial future businesses. “If we flash back to Books A Million and the early Amazon (AMZN) and Pets.com, etcetera, a lot of those didn’t work out, but they did lay the groundwork for what eventually became quite substantial businesses,” he noted.

Addressing MIT’s recent study showing AI could replace approximately 12% of the U.S. workforce, Greenhaus remained optimistic about American job creation.

“I’m, for now, on the other side of all these labor market doomsaying,” he said. “This is America, and we’ve always found a way to create new jobs, new productivity, new labor… and I don’t know why this is going to be the exception to the rule.”

data.title: 10 out of 11 companies beat EPS and revenue estimates this week: Earnings Scorecard
data.description:
U.S. markets were coming off the Thanksgiving holiday and will operate on a shorter schedule on Friday, closing at 1 p.m. ET for Black Friday.

After the Thanksgiving holiday, the futures markets that were opened showed barely any movement.  

Around the time of the European close, futures on the S&P 500 were completely flat, and Treasury futures indicated that yields would only rise by up to a basis point across the curve. 

Earnings Roundup: 

Out of the 11 S&P 500 companies that reported earnings this week, 10 of them surpassed both EPS and revenue expectations. 

Let’s take a look at some of the companies that reported earnings this week. 

Keysight Technologies (KEYS) reported fourth-quarter results and guidance that were well above Wall Street's forecast.

For the period ending Oct. 31, Keysight said it earned an adjusted $1.91 per share as revenue rose 10% year-over-year to come in at $1.42B. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $1.83 per share on $1.38B in revenue during the quarter.

Best Buy (BBY) topped Q3 estimates and set favorable guidance for the crucial holiday quarter. Total revenue rose 2.3% year-over-year to $9.7 billion for the quarter that ended on November 1. 

EPS was reported at $1.40 vs. $1.31 consensus and $1.26 a year ago.

IT and PC giant Dell Technologies (DELL) raised its full-year sales forecast, aided in part by artificial intelligence servers.

For fiscal 2026, Dell now expects revenue to be between $111.2B and $112.2B, up 17% year-over-year at the midpoint of $111.7B, an increase from the prior range of $105B to $109B. 

Analysts had expected $107.94B in full-year sales.

HP (HPQ) offered up a weaker-than-expected forecast and announced it would be letting go as many as 6,000 employees.

For fiscal 2026, HP said it expects to earn between $2.90 and $3.20 per share on an adjusted basis, with the midpoint below the $3.34 per share estimate.

HP said it earned an adjusted $0.93 per share as revenue rose 4.2% year-over-year to come in at $14.64B. A consensus of analysts expected HP to earn $0.92 per share on an adjusted basis, with $14.53B in revenue during the quarter.

Workday (WDAY) reported fiscal third-quarter results and guidance that were above Wall Street's forecast and upped its full-year forecast.

For the period ending Oct. 31, Workday said it earned an adjusted $2.32 per share as revenue rose 13% year-over-year to $2.432B. Analysts had expected the company to earn an adjusted $2.17 per share on $2.42B in revenue. 

Earnings next week: 

Several companies are set to report earnings next week, such as MongoDB (MDB), and Veru (VERU) on Monday, CrowdStrike Holdings (CRWD), Marvell (MRVL), and GitLab (GTLB) on Tuesday, with names such as Salesforce (CRM), Snowflake (SNOW), Dollar Tree (DLTR), and C3.ai (AI) set to report on Wednesday. 

Prominent names like Kroger (KR), Ulta Beauty (ULTA), Hewlett Packard (HPE), and Dollar General (DG) will report on Thursday, with Victoria’s Secret (VSCO) reporting on Friday.

data.title: Beauty, makeup, and appareal top discounted items this holiday season
data.description:
Leading up to the critical holiday shopping season that kicks off with “Cyber Week,” U.S. shoppers were holding back on spending, waiting for the deals that would help offset inflationary pressure and give some relief to price-conscious consumers.

While “highly motivated shoppers are ready to spend, despite rising prices,” said Caila Schwartz, director of consumer insights at Salesforce, retailers in the U.S. are still expected to offer discounts of as much as 37% during the week in apparel, health and beauty, and home furnishings (including appliances).

Salesforce expects the highest discounted categories globally to be beauty and makeup, followed by general apparel, and beauty and skincare, with discounts ranging from as much as 40% to 33%.

To find the best deals, shoppers will rely heavily on AI-powered tools. During the first three days of Cyber Week, customer conversations with AI agents increased 28% versus the week prior.

“Despite headwinds like tariffs and higher prices, consumers are not holding back from holiday shopping,” Schwartz added.

Related tickers: Coty (COTY), e.l.f. Beauty (ELF), Helen of Troy (HELE), Newell Brands (NWL), Edgewell Personal Care (EPC), Interparfums (IPAR), Colgate-Palmolive (CL), Unilever (UL), Estée Lauder (EL), Ulta (ULTA), Sally Beauty Holdings (SHB), Gap (GAP), TJX Companies (TJX), Ross Stores (ROST), Abercrombie & Fitch (ANF), American Eagle Outfitters (AEO), Victoria's Secret (VSCO), Urban Outfitters (URBN), lululemon (LULU), Nike (NKE), Consumer Staples SPDR ETF (XLP), Consumer Discretionary SPDR ETF (XLY)

data.title: CyberArk/Palo Alto Networks deal spread narrows amid Austrian filing
data.description:
The deal spread in Palo Alto Networks (PANW) planned purchase of CyberArk Software (CYBR) narrowed after the deal was filed for approval in Austria. The deal spread narrowed to $5 from $7.27 on Wednesday.

Palo Alto's (PANW) $25 billion acquisition of CyberArk (CYBR) was filed for approval from Austrian antitrust regulators on Tuesday, according to a posting on the regulator's website  dated Thursday.

The deal spread narrowed as the Austrian filing implies that the deal won't need to be filed for approval by the European Commission, traders told Seeking Alpha on Friday.

The deal  won approval from the U.S. Federal Trade Commission in September after receiving an earlier termination notice from the regulator. 

Palo Alto Networks (PANW) agreed to acquire identity security firm CyberArk (NASDAQ:CYBR) for an equity value of about $25 billion in late July.

data.title: Compass Diversified plans to host call for results restatement; stocks gains
data.description:
Compass Diversified (CODI) recently said it plans to host a conference call to discuss the restatement of its financial results. Shares gained 4.6% in Friday late morning trading.

The restatement will be for fiscal years 2022, 2023 and 2024, as well as the unaudited financial information for each of the interim periods included within those years, the private equity firm said.

The call was scheduled for Dec. 4, 2025, at 5:00 p.m. ET.

In June, the company said its financials for fiscal years 2022 and 2023 shouldn't be relied upon amid its investigation of its Lugano Holding subsidiary, with concerns centered on the subsidiary's practices for financing inventory. The disclosure follows its May 7 announcement of non-reliance on its 2024 financial statements.

Earlier in November, Compass (CODI) said Lugano filed for Chapter 11 bankruptcy protection, as CODI continued to work with its senior lender group and bondholders to ensure flexibility.

data.title: Commodity index hits highest level since 2022 as metals rally
data.description:
The Bloomberg Commodity Total Return Index is up 15% year-to-date, hitting its highest level since June 2022, with metals leading the gains while energy lagged amid weaker diesel prices.

"The macro environment offered a constructive tailwind. Global equity markets rebounded after the prior week’s wobble, easing near-term volatility and reducing the risk of dash-for-cash selling across commodities—particularly precious metals," Ole Hansen, Head of Commodity Strategy at Saxo Bank, stated.

Additionally, the U.S. dollar softened as markets priced in a more aggressive Fed easing path. 

Spot gold (XAUUSD:CUR) has risen 4.5% in November, heading for a fourth straight monthly advance.

Expectations for a December rate cut have jumped to around 85% from 30% last week, with markets pricing in three more cuts by 2026—momentum that could bolster metals, including gold, silver, platinum, and copper.

Copper's (HG1:COM) structural tightness story, meanwhile, is strengthening, with forecasters increasingly projecting a deficit by 2026, worsened by more than 60% of visible exchange stocks now concentrated in US warehouses, Hansen said in a note on Friday.

"Idiosyncratic drivers boosted silver and platinum—China’s depleted silver inventories and the launch of new PGM futures—while the energy sector remains weighed down by diesel weakness, even as low crude prices risk setting up the next supply-driven rebound."

Looking at energy, the sector was the outlier with a small 1% weekly loss, increasing the year-to-date deficit to 2.6%. "The sector slipped slightly, primarily due to sharp losses in diesel, while Brent (CO1:COM) and WTI (CL1:COM) were essentially flat, showing little appetite to break out of the tight ranges that have held through much of the past months."

ETFs: (GLD), (GDX), (GDXJ), (IAU), (NUGT), (PHYS), (GLDM), (AAAU), (SGOL), (RING), (BAR), (OUNZ), (SLV), (PSLV), (SIVR), (SIL), (SILJ)

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:USO), (NYSEARCA:UCO), (NYSEARCA:SCO), (NYSEARCA:BNO), (NYSEARCA:DBO), (NYSEARCA:USL).

The Saxo Bank note was published during a major outage at CME; hence, the performance data may not fully reflect the current calendar week.

data.title: Credo Technologies Q2 preview: Strong results expected to further AI momentum
data.description:
Analysts expect Credo Technologies’ (CRDO) second-quarter results to show continued AI momentum, as the company prepares to report the results on December 1.

Earnings per share is expected to increase multi-fold to $0.50 while the company is expected to report a revenue of $234.99 million.

Seeking Alpha analyst Oakoff Investments is bullish on the stock ahead of its results, expecting the firm to build on the resilience semiconductor firms have shown to the AI bubble fears.

“We see that the key AI players are reporting meaningfully above consensus, which is a green flag for CRDO's prospectively beating results, in my view. Also, I don't think that the management will lower their guidance - on the contrary, following the sector's accelerating momentum and the rising AI spending, the odds of seeing another upgrade to CRDO's initial guidance are quite high, in my opinion,” the analyst said.

The management guided for $235 million in sales for Q2 with a midpoint QoQ growth of only 5%. The OPEX should be up to ~$57 million (vs. $54.4 million in Q1), and the adjusted gross margin should be at ~65%, Oakoff Investments noted.

Seeking Alpha analyst Dair Sansyzbayev has also projected a strong second quarter for Credo, driven by strong fundamentals, with triple-digit EPS growth, expanding margins, and impressive execution in the data center and AI infrastructure markets.

In the last three months, Credo has seen 10 upward revisions for its EPS estimates and 9 for its revenue estimates. Meanwhile, in the last two years, the company has  beaten EPS estimates 2 times and revenue estimates three times.

Oakoff Investments expects the positive Q2 results to benefit its stock movement. It has upgraded the stock to Buy from Hold, calling it a “prime buying opportunity ahead of Q2 earnings”. However, Dair Sansyzbayev believes the results may not materially affect the stock movement.

“This earnings season showed us that even Nvidia did not rally after its stellar past quarter's performance. I do not want to bet against the AI momentum again, meaning that waiting on the sidelines with a "Hold" rating is a prudent approach,” Sansyzbayev said.

data.title: UBS forecasts 11% rise for global stocks by 2026 year-end due to AI impact
data.description:
UBS has set a target of 1090 for MSCI AC World Index (ACWI) by the end of 2026, representing an 11% increase.

The investment bank identified artificial intelligence as a key driver, describing Gen AI as “the steam engine of the mind” that will likely increase productivity more significantly than the technology boom of the late 1990s, according to Andrew Garthwaite, chief global equity strategist at UBS.

In addition, the conditions for a market bubble are aligning but have not yet fully formed, according to the UBS analysis.

“We think there is more justification for a bubble (which we are not yet in) to form than any of the many others we have seen owing to the uniquely quick adoption rate of Gen AI,” Garthwaite said, citing at least a 35% probability of a full bubble developing.

Additional support for equities comes from well-behaved U.S. wage growth, historical equity performance after near-miss bear markets, and continued AI sector strength.

UBS research indicated that equity markets have typically risen 43% on average two years after narrowly avoiding a bear market, compared to the 34.6% increase observed so far, suggesting further room for growth.

Garthwaite expects U.S. (COMP:IND), (SP500), (DJI) markets to outperform the rest of the world for several reasons. “U.S. equities are particularly well suited to outperform if we enter the early stages of a bubble,” he said, highlighting that AI investment boosted U.S. GDP by 63 basis points in the first half of 2025.

The bank projects U.S. earnings per share growth of 14% in 2026, supported by expectations that American companies will adopt AI more rapidly than international counterparts.

Lastly, global GDP growth projections have been revised upward by 40 basis points for both 2025 and 2026 to 3.3% and 3.1% respectively.

While forecasting a near-term slowdown, particularly in U.S. domestic demand, UBS identified “clear upside risk” from generative AI, positive wealth effects, and onshoring trends, with U.S. core PCE inflation expected to peak at 3.2% year-over-year in May before declining.

data.title: Ecopetrol plans up to $7.2B capital spending next year
data.description:
Ecopetrol (EC) said Friday it plans to spend in the range of 22 trillion-27 trillion pesos ($5.9 billion-$7.2 billion) in 2026, roughly in line with 2025 levels as it promised "capital discipline for each business line."

The Colombian state-run company said it allocated 17.2 trillion pesos to achieve production of 730,000-740,000 boe/day (89% for crude oil, 11% for gas), planning to maintain investment levels compared to the projected 2025 year-end figures, while maintaining average refinery throughput of 410,000-420,000 bbl/day, and transport 1.11 million-1.12 million bbl/day; crude oil output in Colombia is expected to increase, offsetting natural gas field declines.

The company said it expects to spend 7.1 trillion pesos, ~30% of the budget, on energy transition and power transmission projects, as well as roads and other corporate investments.

Ecopetrol (EC) plans to drill 380-430 development wells 95% in Colombia and 5% in the U.S., and 8-10 exploratory wells, mostly offshore Colombia.

The investment plan was based on a projected Brent crude price of $60/bbl and an average annual exchange rate of ~4,050 pesos per U.S. dollar.

data.title: Intel jumps as analyst says prospects of Apple deal have improved 'significantly'
data.description:
Intel (INTC) shares rose more than 6% on Friday after TF International Securities analyst Ming-Chi Kuo suggested the chip giant could become the foundry supplier for some of Apple's (AAPL) M-series processors.

“My latest industry surveys, however, indicate that visibility on Intel becoming an advanced-node supplier to Apple has recently improved significantly,” Kuo wrote in a post on X. “Apple previously signed an [non-disclosure agreement] with Intel and obtained the advanced-node 18AP PDK 0.9.1GA. The key simulation and research projects (such as PPA) are tracking in line with expectations, and Apple is now waiting for Intel to release PDK 1.0/1.1, currently scheduled for 1Q26. Apple's plan is for Intel to begin shipping its lowest-end M processor, utilizing the 18AP advanced node, as early as 2Q–3Q27, but the actual timeline remains contingent on development progress following the receipt of PDK 1.0/1.1.”

The lowest-end M processors are used in Apple's MacBook Air and its iPad Pro tablet, which are expected to ship roughly 20M units combined this year. 

Kuo, said any potential deal would have positive implications for both Apple and Intel, giving the former a showing of “strong support” for the Trump Administration's “Made in USA” policy. For Intel, the significance of potentially getting Apple's advanced-node business would far exceed the direct revenue and profit from this business. 

“Although Intel will still be unable to compete head-to-head with [Taiwan Semiconductor] (TSM) over the next several years, this suggests that the worst may soon be over for the [Intel Foundry Services] business,” Kuo explained. “Looking ahead, the 14A node and beyond could capture more orders from Apple and other tier-one customers, turning Intel’s long-term outlook more positive.”

Intel, Apple, and Taiwan Semiconductor did not immediately respond to a request for comment from Seeking Alpha.
